description: TCG event-type codes → name, description, and data_format. Used by dev_tpm_tcg to decode TCG_PCR_EVENT / TCG_PCR_EVENT2 records. Covers both the legacy TCG core codes (0x00-0x12) and the UEFI-specific range (0x80000001+). Data format hints guide per-type decoders in dev_tpm_interpret.
source: TCG PC Client Platform Firmware Profile Specification (level 00, rev 1.05+), and UEFI Specification 2.10 §32 (Secure Boot and Driver Signing).

0.name: EV_PREBOOT_CERT
0.description: TCG 1.2 only. Deprecated in TCG 2.0 PC Client spec; should not appear in modern event logs.

1.name: EV_POST_CODE
1.description: Firmware POST code / manufacturer-defined early-init marker. On PCR 0. Typical data: short ASCII string (e.g. 'POST CODE', 'ACPI DATA') or a 4-byte manufacturer code.

3.name: EV_NO_ACTION
3.description: Informational, NOT extended into the PCR. The first EV_NO_ACTION in a crypto-agile log carries the TCG_EfiSpecIdEvent (signature 'Spec ID Event03') declaring which digest algorithms are in use. Other EV_NO_ACTION events may carry StartupLocality or similar markers.

4.name: EV_SEPARATOR
4.description: Boundary marker between firmware-controlled and OS-controlled PCR state. Event data is conventionally 0xFFFFFFFF (error) or 0x00000000 (normal). Extended into PCRs 0-7 once firmware hands off to the OS loader.

5.name: EV_ACTION
5.description: Firmware-defined action marker (e.g. 'Calling EFI Application from Boot Option', 'Returning from EFI Application from Boot Option'). Data is an ASCII string.

6.name: EV_EVENT_TAG
6.description: Tagged event with a 128-bit GUID + variable-length data. Rarely seen.

7.name: EV_S_CRTM_CONTENTS
7.description: Measurement of the Static Core Root of Trust for Measurement's contents (the firmware blob that measured itself). On PCR 0.

8.name: EV_S_CRTM_VERSION
8.description: Version string of the Static CRTM. On PCR 0. Data is typically UTF-16LE text (e.g. 'LENOVO N50HT36W') or ASCII. The most human-interpretable firmware-identifying event.

9.name: EV_CPU_MICROCODE
9.description: CPU microcode update measurement. On PCR 1 (Intel) or PCR 0 (AMD). Data is the signed microcode update header: Intel format is 48 bytes starting with header_version:4, update_revision:4, date:4, processor_signature:4, checksum:4, loader_revision:4, processor_flags:4, data_size:4, total_size:4, then reserved.

10.name: EV_PLATFORM_CONFIG_FLAGS
10.description: Platform-specific configuration flags. Firmware-defined; typically opaque unless we know the vendor format. On PCR 1.

11.name: EV_TABLE_OF_DEVICES
11.description: List of device paths enumerated by firmware. On PCR 1.

12.name: EV_COMPACT_HASH
12.description: Compact hash of some external data. Rarely seen in modern logs.

13.name: EV_IPL
13.description: Initial Program Load. Used generically for OS-loader measurements that don't fit another UEFI event type. Also used by systemd-boot/stub for tag=value measurements on PCR 11/12/13.

14.name: EV_IPL_PARTITION_DATA
14.description: Partition data measured during IPL (GRUB legacy path).

15.name: EV_NONHOST_CODE
15.description: Code executing outside the host CPU (ME, SMC, PSP). PCR 0.

18.name: EV_OMIT_BOOT_DEVICE_EVENTS
18.description: Marker indicating boot-device events have been intentionally omitted. On PCR 4.

2147483649.name: EV_EFI_VARIABLE_DRIVER_CONFIG
2147483649.description: UEFI variable measurement; used for Secure Boot state (SecureBoot, PK, KEK, db, dbx). On PCR 7. Data is UEFI_VARIABLE_DATA: variableName GUID (16B), unicodeNameLength u64LE, variableDataLength u64LE, unicodeName UTF-16LE (unicodeNameLength chars), variableData. For SecureBoot variable specifically, variableData is a single byte: 0x01 = Secure Boot enabled, 0x00 = disabled. For PK/KEK/db/dbx, variableData is an EFI_SIGNATURE_LIST.

2147483650.name: EV_EFI_VARIABLE_BOOT
2147483650.description: UEFI boot-order variable (BootOrder, Boot####). On PCR 1. Same UEFI_VARIABLE_DATA shape as EV_EFI_VARIABLE_DRIVER_CONFIG.

2147483651.name: EV_EFI_BOOT_SERVICES_APPLICATION
2147483651.description: Measurement of a UEFI application loaded during boot services (typically the bootloader PE image). On PCR 4. Data is UEFI_IMAGE_LOAD_EVENT: imageLocationInMemory u64LE, imageLengthInMemory u64LE, imageLinkTimeAddress u64LE, lengthOfDevicePath u64LE, devicePath[lengthOfDevicePath].

2147483652.name: EV_EFI_BOOT_SERVICES_DRIVER
2147483652.description: Measurement of a UEFI driver loaded during boot services. On PCR 2. Same UEFI_IMAGE_LOAD_EVENT format.

2147483653.name: EV_EFI_RUNTIME_SERVICES_DRIVER
2147483653.description: Measurement of a UEFI driver providing runtime services. On PCR 2. Same UEFI_IMAGE_LOAD_EVENT format.

2147483654.name: EV_EFI_GPT_EVENT
2147483654.description: Measurement of a GPT partition table. On PCR 5. Data is UEFI_GPT_DATA (header + partition entries).

2147483655.name: EV_EFI_ACTION
2147483655.description: UEFI boot action marker (e.g. 'Calling EFI Application from Boot Option'). Data is ASCII.

2147483656.name: EV_EFI_PLATFORM_FIRMWARE_BLOB
2147483656.description: Measurement of a firmware blob by its physical address + length. On PCR 0. Data is UEFI_PLATFORM_FIRMWARE_BLOB: physicalAddress u64LE, blobLength u64LE.

2147483657.name: EV_EFI_HANDOFF_TABLES
2147483657.description: Measurement of EFI handoff tables (ACPI, SMBIOS etc.). Deprecated in favour of HANDOFF_TABLES2.

2147483658.name: EV_EFI_PLATFORM_FIRMWARE_BLOB2
2147483658.description: Revised firmware blob measurement with a descriptive blob name. Data: blobDescSize u8, blobDesc[blobDescSize], physicalAddress u64LE, blobLength u64LE.

2147483659.name: EV_EFI_HANDOFF_TABLES2
2147483659.description: Revised handoff tables measurement with a descriptive table name.

2147483664.name: EV_EFI_HCRTM_EVENT
2147483664.description: Hardware CRTM event. Data is the fixed ASCII string 'HCRTM'. On PCR 0. Indicates the platform booted via a hardware-anchored measurement.

2147483872.name: EV_EFI_VARIABLE_AUTHORITY
2147483872.description: UEFI variable authority — specifically, the certificate from db/dbx that authorised the loading of a particular image. On PCR 7. Data is UEFI_VARIABLE_DATA referencing the authority variable.

2147483873.name: EV_EFI_SPDM_FIRMWARE_BLOB
2147483873.description: SPDM-attested firmware blob (device firmware). Rare; appears when SPDM-capable devices are present.

19.name: EV_POST_CODE2
19.description: PFP 1.06 §10.4.2: newer variant of EV_POST_CODE. Data has the UEFI_PLATFORM_FIRMWARE_BLOB2 shape (u8 description length, description bytes, u64 blob base, u64 blob length). On PCR 0.

2147483660.name: EV_EFI_VARIABLE_BOOT2
2147483660.description: PFP 1.06 §10.5.5: revised EV_EFI_VARIABLE_BOOT. Same UEFI_VARIABLE_DATA layout as EV_EFI_VARIABLE_BOOT, but the digest is computed over a PII-normalised view (BootOrder strings and Boot#### descriptions get zeroed). Data decode is identical; only the digest semantics change. On PCR 1.

2147483661.name: EV_EFI_GPT_EVENT2
2147483661.description: PFP 1.06 §10.5.3: revised EV_EFI_GPT_EVENT. Same UEFI_GPT_DATA layout as EV_EFI_GPT_EVENT, but the digest is computed over a GUID/CRC-normalised view (disk GUID and partition entry CRCs get zeroed to allow the same physical disk layout to produce a deterministic measurement across boots). Data decode is identical. On PCR 5.

2147483877.name: EV_EFI_SPDM_DEVICE_BLOB
2147483877.description: Post-PFP-1.06 draft (provisional, not yet ratified). SPDM device firmware blob measurement. Data is TCG_DEVICE_SECURITY_EVENT_DATA2: header + EFI_DEVICE_PATH + SPDM payload.
